claim: The Gandhian independence movement built its sacred political order around anti-sacrificial restraint at the theoretical level: Gandhi's satyagraha doctrine explicitly redirects sacrifice from enemy destruction to voluntary self-suffering — accepting penalty rather than inflicting violence — and claims superiority to body-force precisely because it refuses the sacred-enemy escalation logic. This makes the Gandhian case the clearest theoretical inverse of the pathological sacrificial-escalation cases.

confidence.label: moderate-high
confidence.rationale: Both passages are from Gandhi's own English translation of Hind Swaraj, verified at the Sabarmati Ashram digital archive. The soul-force / body-force distinction is the foundational conceptual claim of the movement's sacred political order. Confidence capped: chain limited to theoretical founding texts; operational episodes (salt march, partition violence) require separate treatment and complicate the clean anti-sacrificial reading.
